statement: Robust structure and substantial tannins require extended cellaring; optimal complexity typically develops after 8 years and can maintain elegance through 25 years with proper vintage selection and storage conditions.
young: Vibrant cherry and dark plum, prominent cedar and warming spice, crisp acidity with firm tannins; concentrated and structured, will mellow and harmonize over time.
ripe: At maturity, layered notes of dried cherry, supple leather, tobacco leaf, Mediterranean herbs, dark chocolate and exotic spice; tannins refined and silky, balanced acidity, extended and nuanced finish.
storage: Maintain consistent temperature of 12–14°C, relative humidity 60–70%, store bottles horizontally; protect from light exposure and minimize vibration.
